seed: control vs surrender; overflow_vs_containment; overfilled_center; stutter_step; hinge against negative_cross; thick_outline; pressure_vs_release; compass; lonely_signal | abstract variation request 500; target batch count 500
title: Tension Between Order and Release
reasoning: The artwork evokes a dynamic interplay between control and surrender. The dark colors on the left symbolize structure, restraint, and confinement, while the lighter colors on the right suggest openness, release, and vulnerability. The central pivot point represents the overfilled center, where oppositional forces collide and interact. The chosen palette reflects both safety and exposure, embodying the nuanced tension of the emotional seed. The central area is dense, creating a feeling of pressure, contrasting with surrounding lighter fields that offer a soft surrender. Broken pixels are intentionally scattered to represent disruptions in the neatness of control, hinting at the complexity of surrendering amid rigidity. This composition resonates with the themes of conflict and balance, navigating between constraining order and the beauty of unguarded release.

layout.name: center_seed
layout.description: A small central symbol is surrounded by pattern pressure.

composition.name: rise_vs_fall
composition.idea: Pixels move upward on one side and downward on the other.

referenceExample.title: Repair After Guilt

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
